title: Commander Fan-out Request (V8.12 #8)
description: Arthur (or a senior tier) issues a Commander Fan-out Request when a task naturally parallelises across N independent items (e.g. analyze 5 PRDs, scan 10 repos, evaluate 3 alternative routes). Each item gets dispatched to an ephemeral worker instance; results return as worker_result objects, and Arthur synthesises.

commander.description: Which agent is acting as commander. Most commonly Arthur for intake/research fan-outs; Marcus for parallel ticket decomposition (V8.12 #6); Magnus when evaluating 2-4 alternative architectural pathways at attempt 19.

worker_role.description: The agent role the workers run as. Ephemeral instances inherit the named role's seed + toolsets but write to a scoped temp scratch dir, never to the permanent home's MEMORY.md.

task_template.description: Prompt template each worker receives, with {item} placeholder where the per-worker payload is interpolated.

payload.description: Arbitrary worker input — typically a file path, URL, ticket ID, or short text blob.

items.description: Up to 20 parallel items. Cap matches Anthropic's Managed Agents 20-parallel-agent limit announced at Code with Claude 2026-05-06.

output_contract.description: Schema file the synthesised output must conform to.

merge_strategy.description: How the commander reduces N worker outputs to 1 final artifact.

max_output_tokens.description: Cap on the synthesised output (separate from per-worker caps).

concurrency_cap.description: Max worker instances running in parallel. Default 5; raise for high-throughput dispatches, lower if budget-watcher is near CRIT.

deadline_seconds.description: Wall-clock budget; workers exceeding it are killed and reported as `timeout`.

scope.description: Lane / project scoping so the commander fan-out is auditable against the project the work belongs to.
